title: The Kid Is All Right

jokes.text: You know, I really think I've made my point.
jokes.setup: Extended musical number about loneliness
jokes.punchline: Lisa breaks the fourth wall to acknowledge the repetitive song
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-comedy that efficiently ends a repetitive musical sequence while staying in character

jokes.text: Oh, the rain has washed away the playground shed! I'm being chased by sports!
jokes.setup: Rainy playground scene
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dramatic overreaction to mundane playground events
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa characterization - her tendency toward dramatic interpretation of normal events

jokes.text: It's called 'precipitation,' 'cause it never fails to precipitate unruly behavior.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining rain scientifically
jokes.punchline: Forced pun connecting weather to behavior
jokes.explanation: Typical Lisa pseudo-intellectual wordplay, mildly amusing but predictable

jokes.text: doomed to get only single pneumonia.
jokes.setup: Lisa talking about friendless children in rain
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'double pneumonia' as worse than 'single pneumonia'
jokes.explanation: Clever medical wordplay that darkly comments on loneliness

jokes.text: A spit wad? Well, at least I'm getting some attention.
jokes.setup: Lisa being hit by spit wad
jokes.punchline: Her desperate reframing of bullying as positive attention
jokes.explanation: Poignant character moment that's both funny and sad, perfectly Lisa

jokes.text: And the best part is I'm bombarding her with her own homework.
jokes.setup: Nelson throwing spit wads at Lisa
jokes.punchline: His pride in using her homework as ammunition
jokes.explanation: Nelson's casual cruelty with added irony about academic achievement

jokes.text: The Autobiography of Charles Manson?
jokes.setup: Lisa looking for comfort in books
jokes.punchline: Finding the most inappropriate book possible
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark humor juxtaposed with Lisa seeking solace

jokes.text: That's a reference to the Bronte sisters! You got my reference to the Bronte sisters?!
jokes.setup: Isabel mentions Charlotte
jokes.punchline: Lisa's desperate excitement over someone understanding her literary reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa characterization - her intellectual isolation makes her overreact to finding a peer

jokes.text: I deciphered the anagrams on your notebook. Oh. Be Lisa? Isabel!
jokes.setup: Isabel explaining how she knows Lisa's name
jokes.punchline: The anagram reveal of Isabel's own name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that serves the plot and establishes both characters as intellectually matched

jokes.text: I finally got that M&M out of my inner ear. I remembered correctly; it was a green one!
jokes.setup: Discussion of friendship
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's bizarre triumph about candy in his ear
jokes.explanation: Pure Milhouse - weird, gross, oddly proud of remembering random details

jokes.text: Don't eat it. It's been in his ear. Don't eat it. It's been in his ear. Don't eat it. It's been in the boy's ear and the dog's mouth. Don't eat it. Oh, for God's sake!
jokes.setup: Family trying to stop each other from eating the ear candy
jokes.punchline: Escalating chain of contamination ending in Homer eating it anyway
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation of disgust with typical Homer payoff

jokes.text: It's raw veggies. They're supposed to be cold! Well, someone who loves you put melted butter on them!
jokes.setup: Marge saying Lisa's food is getting cold
jokes.punchline: Marge's defensive response about adding butter to vegetables
jokes.explanation: Mild family dynamic humor about Marge's cooking efforts

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Someone wants to be my partner. Can't screw this up.
jokes.setup: Isabel suggesting they work together
jokes.punchline: Lisa's internal desperation about friendship
jokes.explanation: Relatable social anxiety moment that reveals Lisa's loneliness

jokes.text: Until the weak-kneed Democrats sold us out at Yalta. If you haven't guessed, Lisa, I'm a Republican.
jokes.setup: Isabel giving conservative talking points during presentation
jokes.punchline: The explicit reveal of her political affiliation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts expectations about Lisa's new friend

jokes.text: A Lincoln Republican? Not really. A Reagan Republican? Keep going. First President Bush? Getting there. Oh, dear God.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to find acceptable type of Republican
jokes.punchline: Each answer getting worse from Lisa's perspective
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation showing Lisa's growing horror

jokes.text: I must say, Lisa, it was amusing to see you thoroughly de-high-horsed. That isn't a word. Ooh, back on the saddle she climbs.
jokes.setup: Isabel making up a word
jokes.punchline: Lisa's correction and Isabel doubling down with more horse metaphors
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows both characters' personalities

jokes.text: I'm a non-observant Jew from Argentina. Ay, caramba! Do you even know what that means? Uh, there-there's a caramba in my eye? It's the Spanish version of 'hot damn.'
jokes.setup: Lisa making assumptions about Isabel's heritage
jokes.punchline: Lisa's embarrassing attempt to define 'Ay, caramba'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Lisa's cultural assumptions and linguistic ignorance

jokes.text: You voted for Reagan?! It was a crazy time, the '80s. You don't hear me listening to the Thompson Twins anymore.
jokes.setup: Lisa shocked about Marge's political past
jokes.punchline: Marge comparing political beliefs to embarrassing music taste
jokes.explanation: Smart comparison that's both character-specific and culturally resonant

jokes.text: All right, '80s party! Where's the beef? Tear down that wall! Because I think the beef is behind that wall!
jokes.setup: Family discussing '80s nostalgia
jokes.punchline: Homer combining unrelated '80s catchphrases illogically
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer non-sequitur mixing pop culture references

jokes.text: No! You never let me be Mike Singletary! Fine. You're Mike Singletary. Enjoy your mediocre coaching career.
jokes.setup: Father-son conflict over who plays which football player
jokes.punchline: Homer's petty revenge through sports history knowledge
jokes.explanation: Petty family dynamics with sports trivia making it specific and funny

jokes.text: Bobby Kennedy worked for Joe McCarthy. Larry David was on Fridays.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining that people's views change
jokes.punchline: Unexpected comparison of political evolution to comedy career embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated reference humor that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: Listen, I'm really sorry about... No, it's okay. My mom says you're just going through a liberal phase. What?! My mom says you're going through a conservative phase.
jokes.setup: Girls apologizing to each other
jokes.punchline: Their mothers both dismissing the other's political beliefs as phases
jokes.explanation: Good symmetrical joke about parental dismissiveness

jokes.text: There's no such thing. Conservatives only get more conservative. Because every year, they get a little further through Atlas Shrugged.
jokes.setup: Discussion of political phases
jokes.punchline: Literary reference explaining conservative political evolution
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with literary sophistication

jokes.text: My father owns a monopoly!
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting they play Monopoly
jokes.punchline: Isabel's matter-of-fact revelation about her father's actual monopoly
jokes.explanation: Efficient joke that reinforces Isabel's wealthy conservative background

jokes.text: She's a young, dynamic Latina. Yee-haw! I said that just right!
jokes.setup: Republican discussing recruitment strategy
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulation for basic cultural sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Political satire about performative inclusivity

jokes.text: He thinks GOP stands for 'Gravy on Pancakes.'
jokes.setup: Discussing Chris Christie's weight
jokes.punchline: Mock acronym based on food obsession
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay though somewhat cheap shot at weight

jokes.text: But despite my mirthful remarks, obesity is no laughing matter.
jokes.setup: Just made a fat joke about Chris Christie
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself with false sincerity
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about hollow political correctness

jokes.text: Just call me Ralph Nader. I don't know why.
jokes.setup: Ralph running for class president
jokes.punchline: Random political reference with no understanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum - random reference with childlike honesty

jokes.text: Sure, Lisa and I dated. Sure, she broke my heart. I'm sure your researchers know all about it.
jokes.setup: Ralph talking about past relationships in campaign context
jokes.punchline: Treating elementary school 'dating' like serious political scandal
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to Ralph's romantic history with political campaign framing

jokes.text: You couldn't buy me with a wheelbarrow full of ice cream.
jokes.setup: Isabel rejecting political corruption
jokes.punchline: Uses childish currency (ice cream) to reject adult corruption
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing her principles through child-appropriate language

jokes.text: You couldn't buy me with a wheelbarrow full of ice cream. Whoa, nicely done. You've charmed another one right into the arms of the Libertarian Party.
jokes.setup: Isabel rejecting Republican help
jokes.punchline: Operative's complaint about losing recruits to Libertarians
jokes.explanation: Smart political satire about ideological recruitment challenges

jokes.text: Most of the money went to convincing them that I was alive.
jokes.setup: Burns claiming he fixed Sexiest Man Alive contest
jokes.punchline: Most effort went to proving he wasn't dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns joke - his extreme age and appearance make this both absurd and believable

jokes.text: Mom, after six weeks, I figured out that Sensei Weinstein is really my psychiatrist.
jokes.setup: Marge mentioning Bart's karate lessons
jokes.punchline: Bart realizing his 'martial arts' are actually therapy
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that works both as parenting commentary and therapy joke

jokes.text: Either way, for 45 minutes, you're not my problem, sweetie.
jokes.setup: Bart revealing his therapy disguised as karate
jokes.punchline: Marge's honest admission she just wants him gone
jokes.explanation: Dark parenting humor that reveals Marge's exhaustion

jokes.text: Idiot! Pick a lane! Wha...?!
jokes.setup: Lisa claiming to be middle of the road politically
jokes.punchline: Bus literally swerving while driver yells about picking lanes
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual metaphor connecting political and literal middle-of-the-road positions

jokes.text: The Supreme Court says spending money is free speech.
jokes.setup: Political corruption in school election
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old citing Citizens United decision
jokes.explanation: Good political satire with age-inappropriate legal knowledge

jokes.text: That ain't rough. More like a gentle breeze. Maggie hits harder. Did the good night pixie just kiss my cheek?
jokes.setup: Bart testing Lisa's pain tolerance
jokes.punchline: Escalating from tough talk to childish interpretation of pain
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's contradictions

jokes.text: Did the good night pixie just kiss my cheek?
jokes.setup: Lisa finally punching Bart hard enough
jokes.punchline: Bart sarcastically minimizes the impact with childish language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart sarcasm - infantilizing language to mock Lisa's attempt at toughness

jokes.text: I associate Isabel with a pain in my tummy.
jokes.setup: Bart's dodgeball strategy against Isabel
jokes.punchline: Student's pavlovian response to getting hit
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy effect with psychological conditioning

jokes.text: The new Wiffle bats? Pizza Friday? Not Pizza Friday!
jokes.setup: Discussion of budget cuts for Lisa's garden
jokes.punchline: Students' escalating panic about losing Pizza Friday
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective escalation about student priorities

jokes.text: We've already had the best plumbers in the city look into it.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying chocolate milk in water fountains is impossible
jokes.punchline: Bart's completely serious preparation for impossible promise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire with absurd campaign promise logistics

jokes.text: Training wheels. She still uses training wheels.
jokes.setup: Bart's opposition research
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing revelation about Isabel's bike
jokes.explanation: Basic embarrassment humor typical of school politics

jokes.text: I'm sorry, you're fired. Leave the bottle.
jokes.setup: Lisa firing Bart as campaign manager
jokes.punchline: Letting him keep his imaginary alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Bart's imaginary drinking with professional firing format

jokes.text: You're a booger head! And I say we move beyond name-calling to solve our real problems. Booger head!
jokes.setup: First grade debate with Ralph calling names
jokes.punchline: Ralph ignoring Lisa's mature response and repeating the insult
jokes.explanation: Typical Ralph behavior contrasted with Lisa's maturity

jokes.text: These are men who fought at Omaha Beach. And they are the reason I'm running for second grade rep. Dios los bendiga a todos.
jokes.setup: Isabel's campaign speech
jokes.punchline: Invoking WWII veterans for elementary school election
jokes.explanation: Good satire of political speech grandiosity at inappropriate scale

jokes.text: Sorry, we're just setting up for An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying 'hang me' after political speech
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation with literary reference
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference with dark humor twist

jokes.text: These are all losers! I'm afraid you're heading for a loss, Lisa. And when we lose, we lose big.
jokes.setup: Democratic party pantheon appearing to Lisa
jokes.punchline: They're all electoral losers with defeatist attitude
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about Democratic party's losing streak

jokes.text: I don't even know why I'm a ghost. All these guys are alive. John Kerry here is Secretary of State.
jokes.setup: Ghost sequence with dead politicians
jokes.punchline: Clinton pointing out the logical flaw in the scene
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor about the show's own fantasy sequence logic

jokes.text: You used to be fun! Kevin Costner used to be a movie star. Get over it.
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about Clinton's political talk
jokes.punchline: Clinton's harsh comparison to faded celebrity
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with good cultural reference and brutal honesty

jokes.text: One brief announcement: the tainted Greek salad has given several students nightmares involving Michael Dukakis.
jokes.setup: School announcements
jokes.punchline: Absurd connection between food poisoning and political nightmares
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd callback connecting food safety to political trauma

jokes.text: It may recur tonight, so if possible, have your parents strap you to the bed.
jokes.setup: Warning about Michael Dukakis nightmares
jokes.punchline: Extreme parental advice for political nightmares
jokes.explanation: Absurd political humor with dark undertones

jokes.text: First grade rep-- results delayed indefinitely due to jelly on the ballots.
jokes.setup: Announcing election results
jokes.punchline: Elementary problem prevents sophisticated democratic process
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between adult democratic ideals and childish reality

jokes.text: Fourth grade rep-- last name Weiner, first name Ima...
jokes.setup: Reading election results
jokes.punchline: Classic prank name that's actually won
jokes.explanation: Old-fashioned prank name joke, predictable but functional

jokes.text: 53% said they'd vote for a liberal. Just not you. Huh. You hear that, world? They love my ideas, just not me! And that is half the battle!
jokes.setup: Lisa losing election but exit polls showing support for her ideas
jokes.punchline: Lisa finding optimism in being personally unpopular
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Lisa's ability to find silver linings

jokes.text: I'd just throw in the towel and make it a state.
jokes.setup: Asked about getting out of Afghanistan
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards solution to military withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Good political absurdity with backwards logic

jokes.text: I'd just throw in the towel and make it a state.
jokes.setup: Future debate about Afghanistan policy
jokes.punchline: Lisa's pragmatic but absurd foreign policy solution
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on complex foreign policy

tmdb_overview: Lisa discovers her new best friend is a Republican who is opposing her in the class election.
